full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic compression-arm (cam-follower lever) for an automated bag-valve-mask (BVM) ventilator per the emergency ventilator benchmark open-CAD challenge Round 1 (MGH/MIT specification). The ventilator uses a motor-driven rotating cam to compress a standard BVM resuscitator bag; the compression-arm is the lever that rides the cam and presses the bag. Ventilator functional specs to honor: tidal volume 400-800 mL, respiratory rate 10-30 breaths per minute, PEEP 5-15 cmH2O, inspiratory pressure limit 40 cmH2O, I:E ratio 1:2 to 1:4. Geometric constraints: arm length 250 mm from pivot to bag-pad; pivot bore 12 mm diameter with bronze bushing shoulder; cam-follower roller pocket 30 mm diameter; bag-compression pad 80 mm x 120 mm curved to match BVM bag; bounding 280 mm x 90 mm x 30 mm. Material: 6061-T6 aluminum alloy (E = 68.9 GPa, yield = 276 MPa, ultimate = 310 MPa, density = 2700 kg/m^3), anodized. Load cases: (R1) Max compression force: bag at 40 cmH2O back-pressure x pad area (9600 mm^2 = 96 cm^2) x 40 cmH2O (40 cmH2O = 3923 Pa) = 37.7 N at bag pad (a small force). Multiply by 8x safety margin for pad-edge stress concentration = 300 N compressive normal at pad. Max von Mises <= yield / 1.5 = 184 MPa. (R2) Cam overrun impact: if motor stalls with arm fully compressed, cam pushes arm at 2x nominal = 600 N; max von Mises <= yield / 1.25 = 221 MPa. (R3) Arm side-load from misaligned bag: 50 N lateral at pad; max von Mises <= 184 MPa. (R4) Fatigue: 30 bpm x 60 min x 24 h x 30 days = 1.3 million cycles at nominal 37.7 N; infinite-life stress limit <= 96 MPa (fully reversed fatigue strength of 6061-T6). (R5) Arm mass <= 250 g. (R6) First modal frequency with pivot fixed >= 30 Hz (to avoid coupling with cam rotation harmonics up to 6 Hz * 5th harmonic). Autoclave steam sterilization at 134 C 0.2 MPa for 20 min must not warp arm > 0.1 mm. Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static x3 + fatigue x1) + modal + thermal. Fluid and buckling NOT required (bag fluid is external; arm not column-loaded). Radiation NOT applicable. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + Parasolid arm, pivot bushing detail drawing, and a report listing arm mass in g, max von Mises per case in MPa, fatigue safety factor, first modal frequency in Hz, autoclave warp in mm.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic cam-follower compression-arm for an automated bag-valve-mask (BVM) ventilator per the emergency ventilator benchmark (MGH/MIT specification), targeting tidal volume 400-800 mL, respiratory rate 10-30 bpm, PEEP 5-15 cmH2O, inspiratory pressure cap 40 cmH2O, I:E 1:2 to 1:4.

Envelope: arm 250 mm from pivot to bag-pad, bounding 280 x 90 x 30 mm; 12 mm bronze-bushed pivot bore, 30 mm cam-follower roller pocket, 80 x 120 mm curved bag-compression pad.

Factor regime: yield FoS 1.5 for the nominal compression, yield FoS 1.25 for cam-overrun impact; 6061-T6 fatigue allowable 96 MPa fully reversed; autoclave steam 134 C / 0.2 MPa / 20 min warp target < 0.1 mm.

Load cases.
- Max compression: 300 N compressive at the bag pad (8x margin on the 37.7 N raw cushion load).
- Cam overrun (motor stall): 600 N at the pad.
- Bag misalignment: 50 N lateral at the pad.
- Fatigue: 1.3e6 cycles at nominal 37.7 N over 30 day duty.
- Pivot-fixed first modal frequency >= 30 Hz.
- Autoclave steam sterilization (134 C, 0.2 MPa, 20 min).

prompt.title: emergency ventilator benchmark BVM compression arm
prompt.brief: 6061-T6 monolithic cam-follower arm for automated BVM ventilator; 1.3 M fatigue cycles + autoclave + 40 cmH2O pressure cap.

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Bag-side fluid is external; reduced to equivalent pad force.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Arm is a bending lever, not column-loaded.

eval_coverage: emergency ventilator benchmark BVM compression mechanism
fatigue life at clinical cycle count
